decision.triage_reasoning: The patient presents with multiple symptoms including fever, muscle aches, fatigue, headache, rash, and joint pain. These symptoms indicate a possible viral or systemic infection, which may require evaluation and treatment within 30-60 minutes to prevent deterioration.
decision.notes: Patient appears stable but has concerning systemic symptoms that warrant further investigation.

decision.context_for_next: Order initial tests for infectious disease work-up.

decision.test_outcome: Hemoglobin: 14.5 g/dL, Hematocrit: 43.2%, White Blood Cell Count: 8,500 cells/mcL, Platelets: 250,000 cells/mcL, Absolute Neutrophils: 6,000 cells/mcL, Absolute Lymphocytes: 1,800 cells/mcL, Absolute Monocytes: 500 cells/mcL, Absolute Eosinophils: 200 cells/mcL, Absolute Basophils: 100 cells/mcL

decision.context_for_next: Need additional tests for infectious disease work-up due to symptoms and current lab results.

decision.test_outcome: Positive 1:100 dilution, White blood cell count: 12,000 cells/μL, Erythrocyte sedimentation rate: 35 mm/hr

decision.context_for_next: We have 2 tests completed so far, which is not sufficient to reach a diagnosis for the patient's symptoms. Given the semi-urgent nature of the case, further evaluation is necessary.
decision.ordered_test: Rapid Antigen Test for viral infections

decision.tests_ran: Rapid Antigen Test for viral infections
decision.test_outcome: IgM: 4.5 index, IgG: 2.1 index, ELISA-Positive, PCR: Positive

decision.diagnosis: Viral infection possibly leading to secondary bacterial infection
decision.recommended_treatment: Antiviral medication and possibly antibiotics depending on further discussion with an infectious disease specialist.
decision.context_for_next: All necessary tests completed and results are consistent with a viral infection accompanied by a secondary bacterial infection.

final_diagnosis.diagnosis: Viral infection possibly leading to secondary bacterial infection
final_diagnosis.recommended_treatment: Antiviral medication and possibly antibiotics depending on further discussion with an infectious disease specialist.
final_diagnosis.context_for_next: All necessary tests completed and results are consistent with a viral infection accompanied by a secondary bacterial infection.
